code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Complete functional failure despite proper usage attempts
    - Multi-purchase defects with safety hazards
    - Usability failures that prevent basic functionality
    - Explicit "avoid purchase" warnings with defect evidence

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Persistent frustration despite occasional success
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - "Regret purchase" statements with justification

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises
    - Target-specific adequacy without endorsement

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only 4 because...")
    - Requires preprocessing/break-in for full function
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction
    - Usage limitations affecting functionality ("don't overstuff")

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Excels in 3+ complementary aspects (function/design/durability)
    - Long-term validation (>6 months) with sustained performance
    - Concise praise without negatives ("Works perfectly")
    - Non-functional criticism with enthusiastic core praise
    - Solves persistent problems from previous solutions
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" despite minor imperfections
    - Outperforms alternatives in key functional areas

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - Functional praise + usage limitation →4
    - Praise + non-functional criticism →5
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - "Better than X" comparisons →5 if core functionality excels
    - Brief effectiveness praise →5 even if concise
    - Core function achieved despite setup →5 if flawless post-setup
    - Resolved issues →5 if no lingering functional impact
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster than first. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures override any positives →1.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Wax and wicks. They work just fine."
<Reasoning>Concise functional praise without negatives →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Stasher bag perfect for snacks. Don't overstuff - affects seal."
<Reasoning>Functional limitation mentioned despite praise →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "This machine foils every cleaning effort - parts never function properly."
<Reasoning>Complete functional failure despite usage attempts →1.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Nearly matches premium brand! Daily driver despite minor differences."
<Reasoning>Favorable comparison with core functionality excellence →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Good for light use. Thin material concerns for heavy loads."
<Reasoning>Adequate performance with notable compromise →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Rollers handle bumps perfectly! Installation took muscle."
<Reasoning>Core functionality excellence outweighs setup effort →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Does the job but constantly needs readjusting. Exhausting."
<Reasoning>Excessive workarounds needed →2.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "These work great after washing smell out. Stiff edges remain."
<Reasoning>Resolved issue with lingering functional drawback →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Vibrant colors need ironing but worth it for durability."
<Reasoning>Non-functional maintenance vs core excellence →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (30 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 15
  gold 5 -> 4: 8
  gold 4 -> 3: 2
  gold 5 -> 3: 1
  gold 1 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "When it comes to cookware cast iron is about as durable as it gets. Yes, it can be a bit harder to take care of (don't throw it in the dishwasher and towel dry it after washing) but it will literally last generations. This skillet came pre-seasoned w..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "This looks like a great idea and easy to use, but actually it's very awkward. The idea is to hang your jewelry in the closet. But the hanger part doesn't swivel, so if you want to select an item, you have to remove it from the closet and lay it on th..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "These not only look great, but they are actually usable. I've bought napkins for my place settings in the past that looks so nice, then I realized they were completely nonabsorbent. So while they looked good, they were pretty much worthless as an act..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I bought these mainly for sending leftovers. It is easier than sending my rubbermaid containers because I am not out much money if they don't come back."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 3, 5] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 30 errors, 17 borderline out of 80 examples."}
